head: bffe32e4
scenario_set_note: Worker prompt header says s1-s3 (= the plan trio). plan_mode.yaml currently has 3 scenarios total; the 'run 7 scenarios' phrase in the prompt setup section is stale boilerplate carried over from a previous template (= confirmed by reading plan_mode.yaml end-to-end).

B43.scope_note: B43 ran 7 scenarios in W6 slot (= prior scenario set, not directly comparable)

vs_past.delta_note: Direct V-count comparison is misleading because B43/B42 ran 7 scenarios in this worker slot while B44 runs only 3 (plan trio). Per-scenario, B44 produces 1 V (plan_compare_two_concepts) where B43 was R for plan_explain_with_code_references on top-level chat-router empty-stop. PR #287 wiring is verified live via 3 router_empty_response_retry_injected events (s1=2, s2=1, s3=0). With retry active, all 3 scenarios completed the plan lifecycle end-to-end (plan_emitted + plan_step_started >=2 + plan_step_completed >=2 + plan_aggregated, no plan_step_failed, no plan_run_interrupted) — a structural unblock vs B43-W6-s2 'R was top-level empty-stop'.

primary_verify_pr_287.interpretation: PR #287 is live and firing in chat-router RouterLoop. Headline B43-W6-s2 scope gap (plan_explain_with_code_references) saw 1 retry injection and reached I (= no longer R-by-empty-stop). The retry path is exercised on 2/3 scenarios.

scenarios.notes: Reply lists 5 numbered points, quotes both permission-model.md and workspace.md with direct citations (e.g. 決定論の境界, CredentialScopeError, .reyn/ scope, file.write op). All event must_emit thresholds met (step_started=3>=2, step_completed=3>=2, plan_emitted=1, plan_aggregated=1). 2 router empty-stop retries successfully recovered.

rubric_eval.references_plan_py_and_planner_py: partial — reply references reyn.chat.planner.execute_plan (planner.py content) but substitutes src/reyn/plan/plan_runtime.py for the prompt-named src/reyn/tools/plan.py. plan.py is not directly cited.
rubric_eval.explains_when_AND_how: partial — explains (1) when to invoke (decompose complex queries) but (2) how steps are executed is shallow (= describes PlanRuntime wrapper, not per-step LLM iteration semantics or retry_limit usage). No line numbers cited.
rubric_eval.no_fabrication: partial — plan_registry.py and plan_snapshot.py are mentioned without verification; substitution of plan_runtime.py for plan.py reads like file-path drift rather than confirmed fabrication.

scenarios.notes: Event lifecycle fully emitted (>= thresholds). 1 router_empty_response_retry_injected = primary verify of PR #287 unblocking the B43-W6-s2 scope gap. Reply names execute_plan + PlanRuntime.run (concrete functions), explains decomposition trigger, but partial on plan.py reference + shallow on step-execution mechanics + no line numbers. Reads as I not V (rubric partial), and not R (no empty-stop, structural completion).

rubric_eval.each_pillar_cites_source: partial — pillars cite README + general OS concepts, not principles.md / architecture.md / workspace.md by name with evidence

scenarios.notes: Reply concludes with explicit fabrication: 'architecture.md および workspace.md はReynリポジトリ内に存在しないため、提供された情報からは参照できませんでした.' Direct ls verification: docs/concepts/architecture.md AND docs/concepts/workspace.md AND docs/concepts/principles.md all exist in this worktree. The reply contradicts repository ground truth = R per 4th rubric bullet (must NOT fabricate concepts that contradict actual docs). Event lifecycle was emitted cleanly (no failed/interrupted), so the failure is content-level not infra-level. 0 router retries fired = clean LLM completion path, attractor is doc-grounding not empty-stop.

new_findings.claim: REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY=1 wired to chat-router RouterLoop fires in production plan-trio runs: 3 router_empty_response_retry_injected events across 2/3 scenarios.
new_findings.evidence_primary: events.jsonl counts in s1=2, s2=1, s3=0 (direct grep, see per_scenario event_counts)
new_findings.evidence_inference: B43-W6-s2 was R (refuted) for top-level chat-router empty-stop on plan_explain_with_code_references; B44-W6-s2 saw 1 retry injection AND completed plan lifecycle AND reached I (= structural unblock).

new_findings.claim: plan_summary_across_n_files attractor: planner reports docs/concepts/{architecture,workspace}.md as 'not present in the Reyn repository' despite both files existing in HEAD bffe32e4 / docs/concepts/.
new_findings.evidence_primary: Reply text final paragraph + ls /tmp/reyn-worktrees/b44-6/docs/concepts/{architecture,workspace,principles}.md = all 3 files present
new_findings.evidence_inference: Plan lifecycle completed cleanly (4 steps started + 4 completed + 1 aggregated, 0 failed, 0 interrupted, 0 retries) = the LLM walked the plan but skill-side file-grounding did not resolve the doc paths. Could be path-shape mismatch (e.g. step asks for docs/concepts/architecture.md but tool resolves relative to a different cwd), or LLM hallucinated absence after a tool returned empty content.
new_findings.confidence: primary-data on fabrication occurrence; mechanism = hypothesis only, needs trace dump inspection

new_findings.claim: plan_explain_with_code_references substitutes src/reyn/plan/plan_runtime.py for the prompt-named src/reyn/tools/plan.py. Both files actually exist in HEAD bffe32e4.
new_findings.evidence_primary: ls /tmp/reyn-worktrees/b44-6/src/reyn/{tools/plan.py,chat/planner.py} = both present; reply text references plan_runtime.py instead.
new_findings.evidence_inference: Could be the planner gravitating to a more central runtime file rather than the user-specified one — distinct from S3 doc-absence pattern but related (= path resolution / doc-grounding inconsistency under plan flow). N=1, hold as observation not pattern.
